title: Fermented Syrian Salmon Crumble
description: A gentle, kid-friendly Syrian-inspired dish featuring tender salmon marinated in fermented yogurt and topped with a crumbly, aromatic breadcrumb mixture. The dish balances mild flavors with a satisfying crumble texture.
instructions: In a bowl, whisk together yogurt, lemon juice, lemon zest, dill, mustard, salt, and a pinch of cumin until smooth.
Pat salmon fillets dry and coat with the yogurt mixture; cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to marinate.
In a small bowl, combine breadcrumbs, crushed nuts, oregano, and a pinch of salt; drizzle olive oil over and toss until the mixture is crumbly.
Place salmon fillets on a parchment‑lined baking sheet and spread the crumb mixture evenly over each fillet.
Bake for 12–15 minutes, or until the salmon is cooked through (internal temp 60 °C) and the topping is golden.
Let the fillets rest for 3 minutes before serving.
Serve warm with a side of rice or flatbread, garnished with extra dill or lemon wedges if desired.
